game_info.title: Untitled Game
game_info.concept: **Octagon 1: Maximal Challenge:** Players control a fixed-position object within an 8-sided tunnel that continuously scrolls forward, requiring precise reflexes to navigate an endless, procedurally generated path. The primary control involves swiping left or right on the screen to rotate the entire 8-sided tunnel, allowing the player to align with an open path and avoid static obstacles like speed bumps. Additionally, players can tap the screen to instantly flip their orientation 180 degrees, effectively moving from the "top" of the tunnel to the "bottom" to bypass gaps or holes in the current surface. Each round in Classic mode lasts exactly 60 seconds, with the game ending immediately if the player collides with an obstacle or falls off the active surface due to misaligned rotation. Difficulty scales by increasing the tunnel's forward speed and the frequency and complexity of obstacle patterns, challenging players to achieve the highest possible score before the timer expires or they fail.
game_info.description: Navigate through an infinite octagonal tunnel that scrolls toward you at increasing speeds. Your avatar stays fixed in the center while the tunnel rotates around you. Align yourself with clear paths to avoid obstacles like speed bumps. Survive for 60 seconds to win, or push for the highest score before time runs out or you crash. The tunnel accelerates and obstacles become more complex as you progress!
game_info.controls:
Left Arrow: Rotate tunnel clockwise (move avatar counter-clockwise around tunnel)
Right Arrow: Rotate tunnel counter-clockwise (move avatar clockwise around tunnel)
Space: Flip 180 degrees to opposite side of tunnel
Enter: Start game from start screen
Esc: Pause/unpause during gameplay
R: Return to start screen from game over

TEST_1.test_description: Tests basic movement mechanics and validates that the player can rotate the tunnel and flip to avoid simple obstacles. Ensures collision detection works correctly and the game responds to inputs properly.
TEST_1.strategy_description: Randomly rotates left or right and occasionally flips when obstacles are detected ahead. Uses simple reactive logic to avoid immediate collisions. Does not optimize for survival time or score.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: The player should survive for several seconds and avoid some obstacles. The test is successful if the game registers collisions correctly, movement controls work, and the game eventually reaches GAME_OVER_LOSE state without errors.

TEST_2.test_description: Implements an optimal strategy to win the game by surviving the full 60 seconds. Tests the complete game flow from start to win condition. Validates that the timer mechanics work correctly and the win state is achievable.
TEST_2.strategy_description: Uses predictive logic to scan upcoming obstacles and determine the safest tunnel segment. Rotates proactively to align with clear paths and uses flips strategically to avoid dense obstacle patterns. Maintains position in the clearest segment based on obstacle density analysis.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: The automated player should successfully survive for 60 seconds and trigger the GAME_OVER_WIN state. The test validates that the win condition is properly implemented and achievable through skilled play. Success is confirmed when gamePhase becomes "GAME_OVER_WIN".
